title: Foundation and Core Integrations
impact: High - Establishes common types and integrates TaskScheduler with core managers

progressCallback.current: Custom or none in most managers
progressCallback.target: Unified ProgressCallback type used by all long-running operations

cancellation.target: AbortSignal support in EntityManager, CompressionManager, IOManager

tasks.title: Create Common Progress and Cancellation Types
tasks.description: Create new file src/types/async.ts with unified types for progress reporting and task cancellation. These types will be re-exported from src/types/index.ts for consistent usage across all managers.

implementation.purpose: Provide consistent type definitions for async operations across the codebase, enabling uniform progress reporting and cancellation patterns.
keyDecisions: ProgressCallback should accept (current, total, message?) parameters
All types should be documented with JSDoc examples

stepByStep.details: Create the file with the new type definitions. See codeSnippets.newAsyncTypes for the exact content.

stepByStep.action: Update src/types/index.ts to export the new types
stepByStep.details: Add export statement: export * from './async.js';
Run 'npm run typecheck' to ensure no type errors and exports are accessible.

acceptanceCriteria: src/types/async.ts exists with all type definitions
Types are properly exported from src/types/index.ts
npm run typecheck passes with no errors
JSDoc comments include examples for each type

tasks.title: Integrate TaskScheduler with EntityManager.createEntities
tasks.description: Add progress callback and cancellation support to EntityManager.createEntities() method using the new async types. This is a critical method used during bulk entity creation.

implementation.purpose: Enable progress tracking and cancellation during bulk entity creation, improving UX for large imports.
keyDecisions: Add optional BatchProgressOptions parameter to createEntities
Report progress after each batch of entities is processed
Maintain backward compatibility - existing calls should work unchanged

stepByStep.action: Import the new async types in EntityManager.ts
stepByStep.details: Add import at top of file: import { type BatchProgressOptions, type CancellableResult, checkAborted } from '../types/index.js';
Change signature to: async createEntities(entities: Entity[], options?: BatchProgressOptions): Promise<CancellableResult<Entity[]>>

stepByStep.action: Add cancellation check at start of method
stepByStep.details: Add: checkAborted(options?.signal, 'createEntities'); after validating input
Replace the single-pass loop with batched processing using batchSize (default 50). After each batch: 1) checkAborted, 2) call onProgress callback, 3) yield control with setImmediate
Return { result: createdEntities, cancelled: false, processedCount: createdEntities.length }
Wrap in try-catch. If Error message contains 'cancelled', return { cancelled: true, processedCount: processed }

stepByStep.action: Update existing tests to handle new return type
stepByStep.details: Tests should access result.result instead of result directly. Add .result to assertions.

stepByStep.action: Add new tests for progress and cancellation
stepByStep.details: Add tests: 1) 'should call onProgress during creation', 2) 'should stop when signal is aborted', 3) 'should return partial results on cancellation'

acceptanceCriteria: createEntities accepts optional BatchProgressOptions parameter
Progress callback is invoked after each batch
AbortSignal cancellation stops processing and returns partial results
Existing tests pass (with minor updates for new return type)
New tests for progress and cancellation pass
npm run typecheck passes

tasks.title: Integrate TaskScheduler with CompressionManager
tasks.description: Add progress callback and cancellation support to CompressionManager.findDuplicates() and compressGraph() methods. These are long-running O(n²) operations that benefit most from progress tracking.

implementation.purpose: Enable progress tracking during duplicate detection and graph compression, which can be slow for large graphs.
keyDecisions: findDuplicates should report progress per type bucket processed
compressGraph should report progress for both detection and merge phases
Return enhanced result with timing and cancellation info

compressGraph.signature: async compressGraph(threshold: number = DEFAULT_DUPLICATE_THRESHOLD, dryRun: boolean = false): Promise<CompressionResult>

stepByStep.details: Add: import { type AsyncOperationOptions, type CancellableResult, checkAborted } from '../types/index.js';
interface FindDuplicatesOptions extends AsyncOperationOptions { threshold?: number; }
async findDuplicates(options?: FindDuplicatesOptions | number): Promise<CancellableResult<string[][]>>

stepByStep.action: Handle legacy number parameter for backward compatibility
stepByStep.details:
At start: const opts = typeof options === 'number' ? { threshold: options } : (options ?? {});
const threshold = opts.threshold ?? DEFAULT_DUPLICATE_THRESHOLD;

stepByStep.action: Add progress reporting per type bucket
stepByStep.details: Count total type buckets before loop. After each bucket: opts.onProgress?.(processedBuckets, totalBuckets, `Processing type: ${typeName}`);
At start of typeMap.values() loop: checkAborted(opts.signal, 'findDuplicates');
return { result: duplicateGroups, cancelled: false, processedCount: duplicateGroups.length };
interface CompressGraphOptions extends AsyncOperationOptions { threshold?: number; dryRun?: boolean; }

stepByStep.action: Update compressGraph to use options object
stepByStep.details: async compressGraph(options?: CompressGraphOptions): Promise<CancellableResult<CompressionResult>>
Phase 1 (0-50%): duplicate detection. Phase 2 (50-100%): merging. Pass progress callback to findDuplicates that scales to 0-50%.

stepByStep.action: Add cancellation support between merge operations
stepByStep.details: In the duplicateGroups loop, check cancellation before each merge: checkAborted(opts.signal, 'compressGraph');

stepByStep.action: Add tests for progress and cancellation
stepByStep.details: Test progress callback receives expected calls, test cancellation stops mid-operation

acceptanceCriteria: findDuplicates accepts FindDuplicatesOptions with progress and signal
compressGraph accepts CompressGraphOptions with progress and signal
Legacy calls with number parameter still work
Progress callback shows two-phase progress in compressGraph
Cancellation returns partial results with cancelled: true
All existing tests pass (with minor return type updates)
New progress/cancellation tests pass

tasks.title: Integrate TaskScheduler with IOManager.importGraph
tasks.description: Add progress callback and cancellation support to IOManager.importGraph() method. This enables tracking progress during large graph imports.

implementation.purpose: Enable progress tracking during graph imports, which can be slow for large datasets.
keyDecisions: Report progress in two phases: parsing (0-30%) and merging (30-100%)
Support cancellation after parsing before merge
Return enhanced ImportResult with cancellation info

currentCode.signature: async importGraph(format: ImportFormat, data: string, mergeStrategy: MergeStrategy = 'skip', dryRun: boolean = false): Promise<ImportResult>

stepByStep.details: Add: import { type AsyncOperationOptions, type ProgressCallback, checkAborted } from '../types/index.js';

Add overload: async importGraph(options: ImportGraphOptions): Promise<ImportResult>;
Keep original signature for backward compatibility

stepByStep.action: Detect which overload is being used
stepByStep.details: if (typeof arguments[0] === 'object' && 'format' in arguments[0]) { // new options style } else { // legacy parameters }
After successful parsing: onProgress?.(30, 100, `Parsed ${importedGraph.entities.length} entities`);
checkAborted(signal, 'importGraph');
private async mergeImportedGraph(importedGraph, mergeStrategy, dryRun, options?: { signal?: AbortSignal; onProgress?: ProgressCallback })
In entity loop: const progress = 30 + (entityIndex / total * 70); onProgress?.(Math.floor(progress), 100, `Merging entity ${entityIndex + 1}/${total}`);

stepByStep.action: Add cancellation checks in merge loop
stepByStep.details: Every 50 entities: checkAborted(signal, 'importGraph');

stepByStep.action: Add tests for import progress and cancellation
stepByStep.details: Test that progress callback receives expected phases, test cancellation returns partial import

acceptanceCriteria: importGraph accepts ImportGraphOptions with progress and signal
Legacy 4-parameter calls still work unchanged
Progress callback shows parsing (0-30%) and merging (30-100%) phases
Cancellation after parsing returns empty result
Cancellation during merging returns partial results
All existing import tests pass
New progress/cancellation tests pass

successCriteria: All four tasks completed with passing tests
Common async types established in src/types/async.ts
EntityManager, CompressionManager, and IOManager support progress callbacks
All three managers support AbortSignal cancellation
Backward compatibility maintained for all existing API calls
npm test passes with all new tests
notes: Backward compatibility is critical - all existing tests must pass
Use CancellableResult<T> wrapper for methods that support cancellation
Progress callbacks should use (current, total, message?) signature consistently
Prefer setImmediate for yielding control to avoid blocking event loop
Test with large datasets (1000+ entities) to verify progress/cancellation works
